results.label: Abdominal pain or cramping
results.context:
General medicine
Adult
validated clinical intent workup
intent: Abdominal pain or cramping
intent_id: abdominal_pain_cramping_v1
intent tags: abdominal_pain; abdomen; abdominal_exam; gi_gu_exam; vitals; vital_signs; infection; hypovolemia; peritonitis; acute_abdomen; appendicitis; biliary_disease; pancreatitis; obstruction; gi_bleed; pregnancy; ectopic; urinary; flank_pain; diagnostic_test; red_flag
clinical bundles: abdominal_gi
required domains: vitals; abdominal inspection; bowel sounds; palpation/peritoneal signs when indicated; abdominal pain initial tests; acute abdomen red flags
avoid labels: PMI; Visual acuity; Vibration sense; Pronator drift

safety.label: Blood pressure
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
safety.management_change: Abnormal blood pressure can change acuity, fluids, antihypertensive decisions, endocrine-crisis treatment, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD

safety.label: Heart rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.
safety.management_change: Abnormal heart rate can change ECG/telemetry urgency, volume or endocrine-crisis assessment, infection severity, medication review, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD

safety.label: Respiratory rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal respiratory rate or labored breathing can change oxygen/ventilation assessment, acid-base urgency, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD

safety.label: Oxygen saturation / support
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal oxygen saturation or rising oxygen requirement can change respiratory support, imaging urgency, pulmonary/infectious/cardiac framing, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD

safety.label: Temperature
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.
safety.management_change: Fever or hypothermia can change infection/endocrine-crisis workup, isolation, medication decisions, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD

history.label: Where is the pain worst, what changed over time, and are there fever, persistent vomiting, blood or black stool, jaundice, urinary/flank symptoms, pregnancy possibility, syncope, severe/worsening focal pain, or inability to pass stool/gas?
history.reason: Abdominal source and acuity: biliary, appendiceal, pancreatic, obstruction/ileus, GI bleed, GU/renal, pelvic/pregnancy-related, infectious/inflammatory, ischemic, or nonspecific self-limited pattern.
history.management_change: The answer changes whether to prioritize peritoneal signs, Murphy sign, CVA tenderness, pregnancy testing, CBC/CMP/LFT/lipase/UA, CT, RUQ ultrasound, pelvic imaging, stool studies, surgical/ED escalation, or serial reassessment.

core.label: Bowel sounds
core.reason: Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.
core.diagnostic_target: Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.
core.management_change: Focal, progressive, peritoneal, obstructive, or distended findings can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, fluid strategy, and serial abdominal exam needs.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

core.label: Abdominal palpation
core.reason: Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.
core.diagnostic_target: Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.
core.management_change: Focal, progressive, peritoneal, obstructive, or distended findings can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, fluid strategy, and serial abdominal exam needs.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

core.label: Abdominal inspection
core.reason: Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.
core.diagnostic_target: Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.
core.management_change: Focal, progressive, peritoneal, obstructive, or distended findings can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, fluid strategy, and serial abdominal exam needs.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

core.label: Abdominal percussion
core.reason: Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.
core.diagnostic_target: Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.
core.management_change: Focal, progressive, peritoneal, obstructive, or distended findings can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, fluid strategy, and serial abdominal exam needs.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

conditional.label: Rebound tenderness
conditional.reason: Makes peritoneal irritation explicit as a conditional abdominal-pain maneuver instead of hiding it until the user supplies perfect localization terms.
conditional.diagnostic_target: Peritoneal irritation or acute abdomen: pain worse on release, guarding pattern, or worsening localized tenderness.
conditional.management_change: A positive peritoneal sign can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, antibiotics, disposition, and need for serial abdominal exams.

conditional.label: Murphy sign
conditional.reason: Keeps biliary disease on the abdominal-pain bedside differential; perform when pain localizes to RUQ/epigastrium or biliary features are present.
conditional.diagnostic_target: Biliary or gallbladder inflammation: inspiratory arrest or focal RUQ tenderness during gallbladder palpation.
conditional.management_change: A positive Murphy sign can change ultrasound threshold, antibiotics/surgical consultation, NPO planning, analgesia strategy, and urgency of reassessment.
conditional.evidence: MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline

tests.label: Abdominal pain initial tests and localization pathway
tests.reason: Abdominal pain needs tests selected by localization and danger features; a generic abdominal exam alone can miss pregnancy-related, hepatobiliary, pancreatic, urinary, bleeding, septic, obstructive, or ischemic disease.
tests.diagnostic_target: Initial abdominal-pain diagnostic data: infection/anemia, renal/electrolyte status, hepatobiliary obstruction/injury, pancreatitis, urinary/stone/pyelonephritis, pregnancy/ectopic risk, inflammatory diarrhea, sepsis, or ischemia.
tests.management_change: Abnormal CBC, creatinine/electrolytes, bilirubin/LFTs, lipase, urinalysis, pregnancy test, stool testing, or lactate changes imaging choice, fluids, antibiotics, surgical/OB/GU consultation, ED disposition, and serial exam urgency.

tests.label: Abdominal imaging pathway by location and risk
tests.reason: Imaging should follow clinical localization and risk rather than a one-size-fits-all abdominal-pain checklist.
tests.diagnostic_target: Imaging target: appendicitis, diverticulitis, cholecystitis/biliary obstruction, bowel obstruction/perforation, abscess, ischemia, renal colic, pelvic/ectopic disease, or low-risk non-imaging trajectory.
tests.management_change: Appropriate imaging can change surgical/IR/OB/GU consultation, antibiotics, NPO status, analgesia, disposition, and need for serial abdominal exams; contrast/pregnancy/renal constraints change modality.

red_flags.label: Acute abdomen and surgical escalation cues
red_flags.reason: A clinically safe abdominal-pain workup must explicitly separate benign cramps from acute abdomen, obstruction, bleeding, ischemia, and sepsis patterns.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: High-risk abdominal process: peritonitis, obstruction, perforation, ischemia, significant GI bleed, sepsis, or high-risk host with subtle signs.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change ED/surgical escalation, imaging urgency, NPO/IV access/analgesia/antiemetic strategy, fluids, antibiotics, type-screen or transfusion planning, and frequency of serial abdominal exams.

red_flags.label: Pregnancy, vascular, and extra-abdominal danger cues
red_flags.reason: Some dangerous abdominal-pain presentations are missed when the checklist stays inside the abdomen; pregnancy, vascular, GU, cardiopulmonary, hepatobiliary, and metabolic mimics need an explicit screen.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: Dangerous mimic or adjacent source: ectopic pregnancy, AAA/dissection/mesenteric ischemia, torsion, ACS/PE/lower-lobe pneumonia, cholangitis, obstructed infected stone, or metabolic crisis.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change pregnancy testing and pelvic imaging, vascular/CT pathway, GU exam/ultrasound, ECG/chest evaluation, antibiotics/source control, specialty consultation, and ED escalation.
